species.distribution: Bhutan, China, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. LUZON: Benguet.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 23 February 2026

species.citation: (L.) Hook. , Gen. Fil. (Hook.) (1840) t.47B ; Copel., FFP 1 (1958) 20; Holttum, Rev. Fl. Mal. 2 (1954) 42; Coritico & Amoroso, Nature Conservation Research 5 (2020) 82; ≡ Osmunda zeylanica L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 1063.
species.distribution: Australia, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Japan, ?Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, ?Thailand, Vietnam. BALABAC, BOHOL (photos), CALICOAN, CAPUAL (photos), JOLO (photos), LUZON: Aurora, Bataan, Isabela, Laguna, NCR, Nueva Ecija, Pampanga, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Agusan del Sur (photos), Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao del Norte, Davao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, Surigao, Surigao del Norte, Zamboanga Sibugay, Zamboanga del Norte, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos), POLILLO, SAMAR, TAWI-TAWI.
species.notes: Throughout the Philippines. Terrestrial in open brushy places near sea level on damp ground. 0m to 100-275m (photos).

species.citation: (L.) C.Presl , Suppl. Tent. Pterid. (1845) 56 ; Coritico & Amoroso, Nature Conservation Research 5 (2020) 82; ≡ Ophioglossum pendulum L. , Sp. Pl., 2nd Ed. (1763) 1518 ; Copel., FFP 1 (1958) 19; Holttum, Rev. Fl. Mal. 2 (1954) 40; Pescuela et al., PJS 153 (2024) 665.

LUZON: Bataan, Batangas, Isabela, Kalinga, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, Maguindanao, Maguindanao del Norte, Misamis Occidental, Misamis Oriental, Surigao del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos), POLILLO, SAMAR.
species.notes: Throughout the Philippines. Epiphytic in everwet rainforests. 1200m.

species.citation: Chantanaorr. & Li Bing Zhang , Phytotaxa 422 (2019) 102 ; Coritico et al., PJS 151 (2022) 228; Type: from Thailand.
Amoroso & Coritico , PJS 150(Suppl. 1) (2020) 216; Type: Amoroso & Nobleza 18966 (PNH, holo; CMUH, BRIT, iso), Mindanao: Bukidnon, San Fernando, Mt Pantaron Range, 820m, 20-Aug-2020.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato.

BOHOL, DINAGAT (photos), LEYTE, LUZON: Benguet, Cavite, Ifugao, Laguna, NCR, Quezon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao del Sur, Misamis Oriental (photos), SAMAR.

species.citation: Copel. , PJS 56 (1935) 97 ; Copeland, FFP 1 (1958) 18; Wieffering, Blumea 12 (1964) 334; Barcelona et al., PJS 142 (2013) 65; Rufila et al., Ruhuna J. Science 13 (2022) 158; Pescuela et al., PJS 153 (2024) 665; Type: Ramos BS 14771, Mindanao: Camiguin de Mindanao.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CAMIGUIN, MINDANAO: Bukidnon (photos), Davao del Sur, Misamis Oriental, PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Note by P.B. Pelser (8-Oct-19): here considered distinct from Ophioglossum reticulatum (Barcelona et al. 2013).

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 1063 ; Copeland, FFP 1 (1958) 17; Wieffering, Blumea 12 (1964) 327; Pescuela et al., PJS 153 (2024) 665. = Ophioglossum nudicaule var. grandifolium R.T.Clausen , Mem. Torrey Bot. Club 19 (1938) 151 ; Copel., FFP 1 (1958) 18; Type: Copeland 1448 (NY, holo), Mindanao: Davao, Todaya, 21-Oct-1904. = Ophioglossum reticulatum forma dilatatum (Miq.) Wieff. , Blumea 12 (1964) 329; ≡ Ophioglossum moluccanum forma dilatatum Miq. , Ann. Mus. Bot. Lugduno-Batavi 4 (1868) 92.
species.distribution: Pantropic. CORREGIDOR, DINAGAT (photos), LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Bataan, Batangas, Benguet, Cavite, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Kalinga, Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao del Sur, Lanao del Sur, Misamis Oriental, Surigao, Zamboanga del Sur, NEGROS, PALAWAN, SAMAR.
species.notes: Probably throughout the Philippines. Terrestrial in humid mostly shaded sites. Only present during the wet season. 0-1600m.
